message:
style: apply rustfmt formatting to scan, update, and error_codes modules

Apply consistent code formatting per project rustfmt configuration.
These are purely cosmetic changes that improve code readability without
modifying behavior.

Changes to scan.rs:
- Break long method chains onto multiple lines for better readability:
  - finding.reason.as_ref().map_or() spans 4 lines for clarity
  - extract_docker_compose_from_str() call uses line continuation

Changes to update.rs:
- Break long error mapping closure onto multiple lines:
  - VersionCheckError::NetworkError construction is now 3 lines
- Format version output statements with explicit line breaks:
  - "Current version:" and "Latest version:" writeln! calls now
    use multi-line format for the interpolation

Changes to error_codes.rs:
- Expand add_context() method signature to multi-line format:
  - Improves readability when parameters have trait bounds
- Break long assertion in tests onto multiple lines:
  - assert!(!code.description().is_empty(), ...) now spans 5 lines

These formatting changes align with the project's commitment to
consistent code style and make the codebase more accessible to
new contributors by following standard Rust formatting conventions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
